No Poverty.reason: Civil law, particularly contract and obligation law, provides the foundational legal framework for economic transactions and resource access, indirectly supporting Target 1.4 on equal rights to economic resources.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to food security, agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to health, disease, or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a required university law course, it directly provides quality tertiary education and professional skills, contributing to Targets 4.3 (tertiary education) and 4.4 (relevant skills for employment).

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives specifically addresses gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course on contract formation and obligations provides the legal foundation for commercial activities and entrepreneurship, directly supporting Target 8.3.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Contract law, a core component of this course, provides the essential legal framework for industrial enterprises and their access to financial services, supporting Target 9.3.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Civil law principles, including those of contract law, establish a framework for legal equality and non-discriminatory practices in transactions, indirectly supporting Target 10.3.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to sustainable cities, communities, or urban development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to responsible consumption and production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to climate action or environmental protection.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course on Civil Law: General Provisions of Obligations is fundamental to the rule of law and ensures access to justice by teaching the legal framework for contracts and resolving civil disputes (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No evidence in the course description or objectives relates to global partnerships, financing, or technology transfer for sustainable development.
